factors.name: Purpose and character of the use
favors_fair_use: transformative, adds new meaning or purpose
parody, commentary, criticism, news, teaching, research
favors_owner: reproduces the original for the same purpose
purely commercial use that supersedes the original

misconceptions.wrong_move: treats any commercial use as automatically not fair use
misconceptions.correction: commercial weighs against, yet a transformative purpose can outweigh it
transformation means a new purpose or meaning, not mere change
this factor rarely controls and usually carries little weight

factors.name: Amount and substantiality used
favors_fair_use: no more than the purpose needs
favors_owner: the qualitative heart of the work even if small

misconceptions.correction: a small but central excerpt, the heart of the work, can cut against fair use

factors.name: Effect on the market
favors_fair_use: serves a market the owner would not enter
favors_owner: harms an established or likely licensing market

misconceptions.wrong_move: looks only at direct sales and ignores licensing markets
misconceptions.correction: harm to a likely licensing market counts

teaching_targets.intended_lesson: a use can look transformative and take little, yet still fail on market harm
a clearly transformative parody can be fair use even when the use is commercial
taking the qualitative heart of a work cuts against fair use even when the quantity is small
